title: Providing amounts for the expenses of the Committee on Homeland Security in the One Hundred Nineteenth Congress.
plain_summary: This bill gives money to the House Committee on Homeland Security to pay for their work during the 119th Congress. The committee can spend up to twenty million four hundred sixty-six thousand dollars total. The money is split evenly between the two sessions of Congress. The committee must follow special rules about how they can spend this money.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.

sections.title: Section 1  Amounts for Committee Expenses
sections.text: The Committee on Homeland Security will receive money to pay for its expenses during the One Hundred Nineteenth Congress. This includes paying all staff salaries. The committee will get no more than twenty million four hundred sixty-six thousand dollars from the House of Representatives accounts that are set aside for committee salaries and expenses.

sections.title: Section 2  Session Limitations
sections.text: The money from Section 1 will be split into two equal parts. No more than ten million two hundred thirty-three thousand dollars can be spent during the first session, which runs from noon on January third two thousand twenty-five until right before noon on January third two thousand twenty-six. No more than ten million two hundred thirty-three thousand dollars can be spent during the second session, which runs from noon on January third two thousand twenty-six until right before noon on January third two thousand twenty-seven.

sections.title: Section 3  Vouchers
sections.text: All payments using this money must be made through vouchers. The Committee on Homeland Security must approve these vouchers, the Chairman of the committee must sign them, and the Committee on House Administration must approve them according to their rules.

sections.title: Section 4  Regulations
sections.text: All money provided under this resolution must be spent following the rules set by the Committee on House Administration.
